title: lycopus europaeus subsp. mollis
intro: Lycopus europaeus subsp. mollis, also known as the soft-leafed bugleweed or woolly bugle, is a perennial herb native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its oval-shaped leaves and spikes of small, white or pinkish flowers. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, making it useful in treating respiratory issues, anxiety, and menstrual disorders. The health benefits of Lycopus europaeus subsp. mollis include reducing inflammation and anxiety, relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, and promoting relaxation. Herbal preparations of Lycopus europaeus subsp. mollis can be found in teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to reap its medicinal benefits.
